title: League of Legends Patch 26.14 Notes
overview: Patch 26.14 is a broad balance pass headlined by an Azir rework to his mana-cost and on-hit mechanics, a batch of scaling buffs to underperformers like Mordekaiser, Corki, Nami, and Yunara, and nerfs to lane bullies and enchanters. Jungle gets stronger with scaling Blue Buff, and Hextech Rocketbelt is reworked into a health-focused build.
summary: Azir's W and R get a big mechanics overhaul — full on-hit damage and better teamfight control.
Mordekaiser, Corki, Nami, and Yunara pick up scaling buffs and hit harder late.
Senna, Seraphine, Garen, and Jayce all take nerfs to their power spikes.
Blue Buff now scales with level, rewarding junglers and mages who control it.

champions.summary: W soldiers now deal full on-hit damage and apply extra rune stacks.
champions.details: Arise! now applies 100% of on-hit effects (Scorch, Liandry's, Blackfire Torch) instead of 50%, double Conqueror stacks, and Press the Attack, while his ult now knocks back jungle monsters — so if you're mastering Azir this patch, your soldiers finally hit like real auto-attacks.
champions.name: Azir

champions.summary: AD growth and rocket recharge both improved.
champions.details: Attack Damage growth goes 2 → 2.5 and Missile Barrage recharge reduction extends to 2-6s, so Corki scales harder and keeps more Big Ones stocked — no excuse for running dry in a teamfight.
champions.name: Corki

champions.details: R true damage drops to 125/200/275 from 150/250/350, so your execute threshold is smaller and you'll need to do more of the work yourself before pressing R.
champions.name: Garen

champions.summary: Passive move speed and ult resistances cut.
champions.details: Hextech Capacitor bonus move speed drops 40 → 30 and Mercury Hammer's armor/MR scaling is reduced, so Jayce is squishier and slower when he swaps forms — less forgiveness for a bad engage.
champions.name: Jayce

champions.summary: E damage/ratio and ult stat steal both increased.
champions.details: Death's Grasp scales to 60/80/100/120/140 with a 45% AP ratio and Realm of Death now steals 13% of stats, so Morde wins his ult duels harder and clears waves faster — bully accordingly.
champions.name: Mordekaiser

champions.summary: Tidecaller's Blessing empowered damage nearly doubled at rank 5.
champions.details: E damage per hit rises to 20/35/50/65/80, so buffing your ADC's autos does real chunk damage now and Nami's poke lanes hit noticeably harder.
champions.name: Nami

champions.summary: Crit modifier and Q AP scaling on slow/heal reduced.
champions.details: Passive crit damage drops to 80% and Piercing Darkness's AP slow and heal ratios are cut, so AP-leaning Senna supports get less utility and your crit windups deal a bit less — lean back into the ADC playstyle.
champions.name: Senna

champions.details: Q AP ratio drops 50% → 40%, so your main poke and wave-clear spell hits softer through the mid game — you'll feel the lost damage every trade.
champions.name: Seraphine

champions.details: AD growth goes 2.5 → 3, giving Yunara a stronger late-game scaling curve so your auto-attacks carry harder the longer the game runs.
champions.name: Yunara

items.summary: Reworked into a tankier build path with a longer active cooldown.
items.details: Build path swaps to Alternator + Kindlegem + Ruby Crystal with 350 health (up) but 60 AP (down) and a 50s dash cooldown (up from 40), so Rocketbelt is now a survivability-first pick — great for mages who want durability over burst, worse if you relied on the fast repositioning.
items.name: Hextech Rocketbelt

systems.summary: Blue Buff ability haste now scales with level.
systems.details: Blue Buff grants 10/15/20 ability haste at levels 1/6/11 instead of a flat 10, so junglers and mid-laners who hold Blue spam abilities noticeably more often as the game goes on — contest it harder.
